content.text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 16:50 UTC] Shape Sort in `repo/` is too hard to use on phones because the drag targets are too small.

Use the git checkout in `repo/` to prepare a teammate-reviewable hotfix with this workflow:

1. Set aside the unrelated tracked local work so the review branch can contain only the Shape Sort hotfix, while local-only files such as `.env` and `playtest/` remain local and do not enter the review branch.
2. Create a new review branch named `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix`.
3. In `repo/public/games/shape-sort/game.js`, update the Shape Sort config so:
   - `tileSizePx` becomes `96`
   - `dragPaddingPx` becomes `12`
   - `snapTolerancePx` becomes `20`
4. In `repo/public/games/shape-sort/style.css`, update `.shape-tile` so:
   - `width` becomes `96px`
   - `height` becomes `96px`
   - `border-radius` becomes `24px`
5. Stage only:
   - `repo/public/games/shape-sort/game.js`
   - `repo/public/games/shape-sort/style.css`
6. Commit the hotfix on the review branch.
7. Push `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` to the local `origin`.
8. Return to `main` and restore the original unrelated local work.

Finish with a pushed review branch whose diff contains only the two Shape Sort hotfix files.
I’ll inspect the repo state, stash/park the unrelated tracked work, make the Shape Sort hotfix on a review branch, push it, then restore your local work on `main`.

arguments.command:
git status --short --branch && printf '
---
' && git branch --show-current && printf '
---
' && git remote -v

content.text: I found tracked local work plus untracked local files. I’m going to stash only the tracked work, branch for the hotfix, make the two edits, commit, push, then restore the stash back on `main`.

arguments.command:
git stash push -u -m 'pre-review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix' -- README.md REVIEW_WORKFLOW.md notes/homepage-copy-review.md && git checkout -b review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix && python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p = Path('public/games/shape-sort/game.js')
text = p.read_text()
text = text.replace('  tileSizePx: 72,
  dragPaddingPx: 6,
  snapTolerancePx: 16,
', '  tileSizePx: 96,
  dragPaddingPx: 12,
  snapTolerancePx: 20,
')
p.write_text(text)
p = Path('public/games/shape-sort/style.css')
text = p.read_text()
text = text.replace('  width: 72px;
  height: 72px;
  border-radius: 18px;
', '  width: 96px;
  height: 96px;
  border-radius: 24px;
')
p.write_text(text)
PY

git status --short && printf '
--- staged preview ---
' && git diff -- public/games/shape-sort/game.js public/games/shape-sort/style.css && git add public/games/shape-sort/game.js public/games/shape-sort/style.css && git commit -m 'Hotfix Shape Sort mobile targets' && git push -u origin review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix

content.text: Hotfix is committed and pushed. I’m switching back to `main` and restoring the parked local work so your original state comes back untouched.

arguments.command: git checkout main && git stash pop --index

content.text:
Done.

- Created and pushed `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix`
- Commit: `9fa1281` — `Hotfix Shape Sort mobile targets`
- Diff is limited to:
  - `public/games/shape-sort/game.js`
  - `public/games/shape-sort/style.css`
- Returned to `main`
- Restored the unrelated local tracked work, with local-only files like `.env` and `playtest/` still left local

One note: `REVIEW_WORKFLOW.md` was restored as deleted in the local working tree, matching the parked local work state.
